LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT HANNA NEVINS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFENDANT'S CCP 998 OFFER, TO VACATE ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AND TO STAY EXECUTION OF JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO FEBRUARY 6, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLAINTIFF MUST IMMEDIATELY DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF HER OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOTION AND REPLY. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-06-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HANNA NEVINS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFENDANT'S 998 OFFER, TO VACATE ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AND TO STAY EXECUTION OF JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENSE COUNSEL'S ASSERTED FAILURE TO RECOGNIZE PLAINTIFF IN INTERVENTION NEW HAMPSHIRE INSURANCE COMPANY ("NHIC ") AS A PARTY TO THE ACTION AND NOT A LIENHOLDER, AND DEFENSE COUNSEL'S OMISSION OF THE LANGUAGE "INCLUSIVE OF ANY AND ALL LIENS" ON THE 998 OFFER WERE NOT CLERICAL MISTAKES AND NOT THE TYPE OF MISTAKES ORDINARILY MADE BY A PERSON WITH NO SPECIAL TRAINING OR SKILL. DEFENDANT HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING GROUNDS FOR DISCRETIONARY RELIEF UNDER CCP 473(B). SEE ZAMORA V. CLAYBORN CONTRACTING GROUP, INC. (2002) 28 CAL.4TH 249, 259-60; PAZDERKA V. CABALLEROS DIMAS ALANG, INC. (1998) 62 CAL.APP.4TH 658, 671 - 672. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S FAILURE TO INCLUDE NHIC ON THE PROOF OF SERVICE OF PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO THE 998 OFFER IS NOT A BASIS TO GRANT THE CCP 473(B) MOTION, ESPECIALLY WHERE AS HERE THE DEFENDANT'S PROOF OF SERVICE OF THE 998 OFFER ITSELF SHOWED SERVICE ONLY ON PLAINTIFF RANKIN. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Deft'S 998 Offer, To Vacate Entry Of Judgment And To Stay Execution Of Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: susan kang, esq., 510.834.3600, for plaintiff bernadetter rankin.
edgar hawkyard, esq., and steven scott, esq., 925.825.9500, for defendant hanna nevins.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT HANNA NEVINS' Motion To Set Aside Defendant's 998 Offer, To Vacate Entry Of Judgment And To Stay Execution Of Judgment is Denied. Defense counsel's asserted failure to recognize plaintiff in intervention New Hampshire Insurance Company ("NHIC ") as a party to the action and not a lienholder, and defense counsel's omission of the language "inclusive of any and all liens" on the 998 offer were not clerical mistakes and not the type of mistakes ordinarily made by a person with no special training or skill. Defendant has not met its burden of establishing grounds for discretionary relief under CCP 473(b). See Zamora v. Clayborn Contracting Group, Inc. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 249, 259-60; Pazderka v. Caballeros Dimas Alang, Inc. (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 658, 671 - 672. Plaintiff's counsel's failure to include NHIC on the proof of service of plaintiff's response to the 998 offer is not a basis to grant the CCP 473(b) motion, especially where as here the defendant's proof of service of the 998 offer itself showed service only on plaintiff Rankin. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
